secondary_archetype: Forward-deployed / Solutions PM with legal domain focus

level_signals: Comp range $178,500–$241,500 base suggests mid-to-senior IC
Role requires independently running multiple client engagements in parallel
Blend of product management, innovation strategy, and forward-deployed partnership implies seniority

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have experience at a top law firm or in-house legal team
Are product-savvy and tech-curious. You're not expected to code, but you can navigate conversations about generative AI, workflow design, privacy, governance, and enterprise rollout.
Communicate with clarity and confidence across roles — partners, associates, engineers, PMs, CIOs, and legal ops.
Can independently run multiple client engagements in parallel.
Care deeply about quality and user outcomes while keeping a strong bias for action.
Are energized by building something new in a domain going through its biggest shift in decades.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: innovation, legal ops, or consulting experience is a plus.
Familiarity with the legal tech ecosystem as an employee, founder, or advisor to legal tech startups

scope.surface: AI-powered legal workflows for top global law firms and corporate legal teams
scope.business_model: enterprise B2B SaaS for legal and professional services
primary_metrics_inferred: Platform embeddedness in day-to-day legal work
Business impact of AI (associate leverage, workflow transformation)
core_responsibilities: Serve as a thought partner to top global law firms and enterprises — diagnosing pain points, mapping high-value opportunities, and guiding how Harvey integrates into their practice.
Work with the go-to-market team to shape pilots and deployments end-to-end: discovery, workflow design, testing, onboarding, iteration, and firm-wide rollout.
Work with innovation leaders, legal operations, and practice groups alongside Customer Success — ensuring the platform becomes embedded in day-to-day legal work
Translate user insights into product direction to influence roadmap
Help clients evaluate the business impact of AI — from improving associate leverage to transforming workflows across practices like M&A, litigation, regulatory, and transactions.
Shape how the legal industry adapts to AI.

green_flags: Clear product surface and customer segments described (top global law firms and corporate legal teams)

Well-funded, high-growth company at early stage with world-class investor support
Role blends unique mix of domain depth and product innovation — differentiated mandate
